user_scenarios.user_goal: As a remote crawler workbench operator, validate the integrated backend and frontend domain-channel workflow in an isolated local deployment.
prerequisites: Backend and frontend dependencies are installed.
The evaluator can bind isolated local backend and frontend ports.
No long-running developer backend or frontend service is reused.
No real credentials are required; only synthetic test secrets may be used.
steps: Start an isolated crawler workbench backend with a temporary state directory and scheduler disabled.
Start an isolated Vite frontend bound to 0.0.0.0 or a safe local test host as required by the evaluator.
Use the API to create a channel, attach a source, write a synthetic secret, run a local HTTP probe, and fetch probe history.
Use Playwright against the real Vite UI to open Domain Channels, view the created channel, replace a synthetic secret, create a source, and inspect latest probe status.
Run an existing source-flow fixture to confirm raw evidence and queue continuity still work.
Write evaluator result, summary, and browser evidence under the output directory.
Redact Playwright JSON/report diagnostics that include synthetic secret environment values, then scan retained artifacts for forbidden plaintext.
expected_outcomes: Backend and frontend start from isolated state and do not reuse long-running services.
Channel and source API workflows succeed.
The UI shows the created channel and source.
Synthetic secret plaintext is not present in retained artifacts.
Source run, raw item, and queue continuity remain intact for the fixture.
Evaluator writes pass results for wiki crawler continuity, source subscriptions UI, and Domain Channels live UI.
failure_signals: The evaluator connects to an old backend or frontend service.
Channel API endpoints are missing or fail in the integrated environment.
The UI cannot complete the Domain Channels flow.
Synthetic secret plaintext appears in retained logs, traces, screenshots, or JSON artifacts.
Existing crawler source-run and queue continuity regress.
cleanup: Stop isolated backend and frontend processes.
Remove temporary state directories created by the evaluator.
